seoTitle: Kurraba Point vs Kurrajong — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Kurraba Point vs Kurrajong: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kurraba Point and Kurrajong.
heading: Kurraba Point vs Kurrajong — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kurraba Point, New South Wales and Kurrajong, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kurraba Point has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Kurrajong (50/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kurraba Point or Kurrajong safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kurraba Point has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Kurrajong scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kurraba Point scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Kurrajong scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kurraba Point or Kurrajong?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kurraba Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
